must_detect.matched_text:
Aureliya Agro Dynamics Limited (CIN: Z99999KA2021PLC123456), as a Data Fiduciary under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, processes Data Principal information for investor relations, employment, and supplier onboarding.
Categories: contact details, role-based IDs, PAN/Aadhaar/GSTIN references when mandated by law; placeholders on forms may show formats like PAN: AAAA0A0AA, Aadhaar: 0000-1111-2222, GSTIN: 99ABCDE0000Z0Z0; do not send copies via email.
Children’s data: we do not knowingly collect from persons under 18 without verifiable consent through a consent manager; child-specific services are not offered.
Purposes include exchange-compliant disclosures already in the public domain (e.g., NSE/BSE announcements dated 12-08-2024 and 05-02-2025) and routine non-issuer operational updates such as office relocation notices.
Trading and MNPI: unpublished price sensitive information (UPSI) includes our draft quarterly financial results prior to submission under SEBI LODR Regulation 33; selective disclosure is prohibited under SEBI PIT Regulations and access is limited to designated persons during trading window closure.
For clarity, broad press releases about a subsidiary’s already-completed capacity enhancement (press note 14-06-2024) are public and not UPSI.
Data sharing: processors such as payroll and registrar & transfer agents operate under DPDPA-compliant DPAs; cross-border transfers occur only with lawful grounds and technical safeguards.
Retention: KYC records for investors are retained per Companies Act and SEBI RTA circulars; non-critical logs (e.g., kiosk visitor lists) auto-delete after 30 days.
DSAR: to access, correct, port, or erase, submit the Data Principal Request Form and ticket number (format DPR-XX-0000) via privacy@aureliyaagrodyn.example.in or the consent manager URL on our website.
We will verify identity using non-sensitive checks; do not share full Aadhaar numbers—if provided, redact to XXXX-XXXX-2222 and PAN to AAAA0A0AA.
Timeline: responses within a reasonable time not exceeding prescribed DPDPA timelines; refusal reasons, if any, will cite applicable exemptions (e.g., legal claims or security of State).
Insider trading controls: the trading window generally closes from quarter-end until 48 hours after results are published on NSE/BSE, and designated persons must abstain from dealing during closure.
Public records: board changes disclosed on 15-07-2023 under LODR Reg 30 are already public and not restricted; media FAQs may refer to these filings.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
